message.content:
There are two big problems that remain:

1. The page restoration. Even though we store chapter and page inside local storage, sometimes the _wrong_ page is restored, like always going one page further, or one page back. I think this has to do with the snap to page and or the animation timing. Maybe something else though.

2. When going backwards, to previous pages, when I hit a previous chapter the transition sucks because the new chapter gets loaded then the animation to the last page of the chapter. It might be better when going backwards to somehow disable that scroll animation. I don't know if a fade in would be better, or just have it immediately show.

1→# Plan: Flesh Out Flatland Example
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→The Pressy framework packages are all implemented, but the `examples/flatland/` directory is minimal — just a preface chapter. We need a complete working example that exercises the framework's features: multiple chapters with navigation, MDX components (Aside, Footnote, Figure, Callout, SceneBreak), an article, and theme customization.
     6→
     7→The plan specifies using *Flatland* by Edwin A. Abbott (public domain, Project Gutenberg) as test content.
     8→
     9→## What Already Exists
    10→
    11→- `examples/flatland/package.json` — deps on pressy, @pressy/components, @pressy/typography
    12→- `examples/flatland/pressy.config.ts` — configured for paginated mode
    13→- `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/_book.yaml` — book metadata (paywall disabled)
    14→- `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/00-preface.mdx` — one chapter using `<Aside>`
    15→- `examples/flatland/theme/` — empty directory
    16→
    17→## Plan
    18→
    19→### 1. Add Flatland chapters (Part I: This World)
    20→
    21→Create ~6 chapters from Part I of Flatland, each using several MDX components to exercise the framework. Content sourced from Project Gutenberg (public domain).
    22→
    23→Files to create:
    24→- `content/books/flatland/01-of-the-nature-of-flatland.mdx` — Ch 1, uses `<Aside>`, `<Callout>`
    25→- `content/books/flatland/02-of-the-climate-and-houses.mdx` — Ch 2, uses `<Footnote>`, `<SceneBreak>`
    26→- `content/books/flatland/03-concerning-the-inhabitants.mdx` — Ch 3, uses `<Aside>`, `<Footnote>`
    27→- `content/books/flatland/04-concerning-the-women.mdx` — Ch 4, uses `<Callout type="warning">`, `<SceneBreak>`
    28→- `content/books/flatland/05-of-our-methods-of-recognizing.mdx` — Ch 5, uses `<Footnote>`, `<Aside>`
    29→- `content/books/flatland/06-of-recognition-by-sight.mdx` — Ch 6, uses `<Callout type="tip">`, `<SceneBreak>`
    30→
    31→Each chapter will have:
    32→- Proper frontmatter with `title`
    33→- An h1 matching the title
    34→- Authentic Flatland text adapted to MDX
    35→- 2-3 MDX component usages woven naturally into the content
    36→
    37→### 2. Add Part II intro chapter
    38→
    39→- `content/books/flatland/07-concerning-a-stranger.mdx` — Start of Part II, uses `<Callout type="important">`, `<Aside>`, demonstrates a part transition
    40→
    41→### 3. Add an article example
    42→
    43→Create `content/articles/about-flatland/` to demonstrate the articles feature:
    44→- `_article.yaml` — article metadata with tags
    45→- `index.mdx` — a short "About This Edition" article using `<Callout>` and `<Footnote>`
    46→
    47→### 4. Add theme customization
    48→
    49→- `theme/custom.css` — minimal custom CSS showing how to override typography defaults (e.g., custom drop cap color, a custom font import)
    50→
    51→### 5. Add a placeholder cover
    52→
    53→- `content/books/flatland/cover.svg` — a simple SVG cover image (geometric shapes fitting the Flatland theme)
    54→
    55→## Files to Create/Modify
    56→
    57→| File | Action |
    58→|------|--------|
    59→| `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/01-of-the-nature-of-flatland.mdx` | Create |
    60→| `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/02-of-the-climate-and-houses.mdx` | Create |
    61→| `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/03-concerning-the-inhabitants.mdx` | Create |
    62→| `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/04-concerning-the-women.mdx` | Create |
    63→| `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/05-of-our-methods-of-recognizing.mdx` | Create |
    64→| `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/06-of-recognition-by-sight.mdx` | Create |
    65→| `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/07-concerning-a-stranger.mdx` | Create |
    66→| `examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/cover.svg` | Create |
    67→| `examples/flatland/content/articles/about-flatland/_article.yaml` | Create |
    68→| `examples/flatland/content/articles/about-flatland/index.mdx` | Create |
    69→| `examples/flatland/theme/custom.css` | Create |
    70→
    71→## Verification
    72→
    73→1. Run `pnpm install` from the monorepo root
    74→2. Run `pnpm --filter flatland-example dev` to start the dev server
    75→3. Verify:
    76→   - Home page lists the book and article
    77→   - Book page shows all 8 chapters (preface + 7)
    78→   - Chapter navigation (prev/next) works
    79→   - MDX components render (Aside, Footnote, Callout, SceneBreak)
    80→   - Paginated mode works (configured as default)
    81→   - Theme switcher works
    82→   - Article page renders
    83→   - Custom theme CSS applies
    84→

1→export interface SiteConfig {
     2→  title: string
     3→  url: string
     4→  description?: string
     5→  author?: string
     6→  language?: string
     7→}
     8→
     9→export interface PaginationConfig {
    10→  defaultMode: 'scroll' | 'paginated'
    11→}
    12→
    13→export interface ShopifyConfig {
    14→  shopDomain: string
    15→  storefrontAccessToken: string
    16→}
    17→
    18→export interface PWAConfig {
    19→  /** Enable PWA support (service worker, manifest, offline). Defaults to true. */
    20→  enabled?: boolean
    21→  /** Short name for the app (used on home screen). Defaults to site.title. */
    22→  shortName?: string
    23→  /** Theme color for the browser chrome. Defaults to '#ffffff'. */
    24→  themeColor?: string
    25→  /** Background color for splash screen. Defaults to '#ffffff'. */
    26→  backgroundColor?: string
    27→  /** Display mode. Defaults to 'standalone'. */
    28→  display?: 'standalone' | 'minimal-ui' | 'fullscreen' | 'browser'
    29→  /** Path to a custom icon (used for both 192x192 and 512x512). Overridden by icon192/icon512 if set. */
    30→  icon?: string
    31→  /** Path to a custom 192x192 icon. */
    32→  icon192?: string
    33→  /** Path to a custom 512x512 icon. */
    34→  icon512?: string
    35→  /** Path to a custom favicon. Falls back to icon192 or icon if not set. */
    36→  favicon?: string
    37→}
    38→
    39→export interface PressyConfig {
    40→  site: SiteConfig
    41→  pagination?: PaginationConfig
    42→  shopify?: ShopifyConfig
    43→  pwa?: PWAConfig
    44→  outDir?: string
    45→  contentDir?: string
    46→}
    47→
    48→export function defineConfig(config: PressyConfig): PressyConfig {
    49→  return {
    50→    pagination: {
    51→      defaultMode: 'scroll',
    52→    },
    53→    pwa: {
    54→      enabled: true,
    55→      themeColor: '#ffffff',
    56→      backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
    57→      display: 'standalone',
    58→    },
    59→    outDir: 'dist',
    60→    contentDir: 'content',
    61→    ...config,
    62→  }
    63→}
    64→

Excellent! Now I have comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document:

## Comprehensive Summary: Pressy Web Framework Codebase

Based on my thorough exploration of the Pressy web framework for self-publishing, here's a comprehensive analysis of the key areas you requested:

### **1. Overall Project Structure**

**Key Files:**
- `/Users/blittle/dev/pressy/packages/pressy/src/` - Main framework package
  - `runtime/client.tsx` - Client-side hydration and state management
  - `runtime/offline.ts` - Offline/PWA functionality
  - `vite/plugin.ts` - Vite plugin for build-time processing
  - `types.ts` - Type definitions
  - `config.ts` - Configuration types

- `/Users/blittle/dev/pressy/packages/@pressy/components/src/` - UI components
  - `Reader.tsx` - Main reading interface (scroll and paginated modes)
  - `Navigation.tsx` - Chapter navigation UI
  - `BookProgress.tsx` - Progress tracking display
  - `content/` - MDX content components (Aside, Footnote, Figure, Callout, SceneBreak)

---

### **2. Pages and Navigation System**

**Page Structure in Paginated Mode:**
The framework uses CSS multi-column layout for pagination:

- **File:** `/Users/blittle/dev/pressy/packages/@pressy/components/src/Reader.tsx` (lines 2542-2556)
- Each "page" is one CSS column
- `column-width` is set dynamically via JavaScript to match viewport width
- Content overflows horizontally into new columns
- Total pages calculated: `Math.max(1, Math.round(article.scrollWidth / viewportWidth))` (line 925)

**Chapter Transitions:**

1. **Forward Navigation (next chapter):** Lines 1338-1347
   - At last page: navigates to `effectiveNextChapter.slug`
   - Otherwise: increments current page

2. **Backward Navigation (previous chapter):** Lines 1349-1358
   - At first page: navigates to `effectivePrevChapter.slug` with `?page=last` query param
   - Otherwise: decrements current page
   - Special handling for multi-chapter mode (lines 1313-1328)

3. **Multi-Chapter Preloading:** Lines 1107-1162
   - When within 2 pages of chapter end, preload next chapter dynamically
   - Uses `chapterMapData` to lazy-load chapter components
   - Preloading tracked in `preloadingRef` to avoid duplicates

4. **Chapter Change Detection:** Lines 1164-1207
   - `updateChapterRanges()` builds mapping of which pages belong to which chapter
   - Automatically updates URL using `history.replaceState()` (line 1178)
   - Updates document title (lines 1185-1188)
   - Calls `onChapterChange()` callback to save progress for the chapter being left

---

### **3. Snap-to-Page Logic and Scroll Behavior**

**Page Snapping (Paginated Mode):**

1. **Page Position Calculation:** Lines 1044-1063
   - Base offset: `currentPage * viewport.clientWidth`
   - Drag offset subtracted: `baseOffset - dragOffset`
   - Applied via CSS transform: `translateX(-${totalOffset}px)`

2. **Transition Timing:**
   - **During drag:** No transition (immediate response)
   - **On snap:** `transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94)` - ease-out curve (lines 1054-1060)
   - CSS comment at lines 2542-2545 explains the layout

3. **Viewport Height Sync:** Lines 835-854
   - Uses `window.innerHeight` instead of `100dvh` (unreliable on Android PWAs)
   - Synced to CSS variable `--app-height` via ResizeObserver
   - Also listens to `visualViewport` events for virtual keyboard changes

4. **Scroll Reader (Scroll Mode):** Lines 209-235
   - Uses normal scroll position restoration
   - Saves/restores `scrollPosition` from localStorage (line 341)
   - Debounced progress saving (lines 347-349)

---

### **4. LocalStorage Page Restoration**

**Storage Strategy (Dual Approach):**

**synchronous localStorage (Single Source of Truth for Position Restore):**
- **Key:** `'pressy-last-read'`
- **Data structure:** 
  ```json
  {
    "bookSlug": string,
    "chapterSlug": string,
    "page": number,
    "totalPages": number,
    "scrollPosition": number
  }
  ```
- **Writes:** Lines 412-416, 464-467 (synchronous on every save)
- Survives page close/refresh reliably

**Asynchronous IndexedDB (Per-Chapter History):**
- **Database:** `'pressy'` (version 1)
- **Store:** `'reading-progress'`
- **Data:** `ReadingProgress` interface (line 69-75 in types.ts)
- **Purpose:** Per-chapter history for book progress calculation
- May not flush before close, but fine for progress %

**Auto-Redirect on Fresh App Open:** Lines 790-816
```typescript
const isNewSession = !sessionStorage.getItem('pressy-session-active')
sessionStorage.setItem('pressy-session-active', '1')
// On visibility hidden, clear session flag so next foreground = fresh open
document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => {
  if (document.visibilityState === 'hidden') {
    sessionStorage.removeItem('pressy-session-active')
  }
})

if (isNewSession && (data.routeType === 'home' || data.routeType === 'book')) {
  const lastRead = localStorage.getItem('pressy-last-read')
  if (lastRead) {
    // Parse and redirect to chapter if valid
    window.location.replace(`${basePath}/books/${bookSlug}/${chapterSlug}`)
  }
}
```

**Page Restoration in Paginated Mode:** Lines 1015-1042
```typescript
// Called after totalPages computed
onRestoreProgress().then((data) => {
  if (!data || data.page <= 0) return
  
  let targetPage: number
  if (data.totalPages === totalPages) {
    // Same layout — restore exact page
    targetPage = data.page
  } else if (data.totalPages > 0) {
    // Viewport changed — scale proportionally
    const fraction = data.page / (data.totalPages - 1)
    targetPage = Math.round(fraction * (totalPages - 1))
  }
  
  const clamped = Math.max(0, Math.min(targetPage, totalPages - 1))
  setCurrentPage(clamped)
  hasRestoredRef.current = true
})
```

**Progress Saving:** Lines 1209-1249
- Debounced 300ms (line 1237)
- Relative to chapter in multi-chapter mode (lines 1220-1222)
- Also saved on page unload/visibility change (lines 1251-1297)

---

### **5. Animation and Transition Code**

**Main Snap Animation:** Lines 1054-1062
```typescript
if (isDragging) {
  article.style.transition = "none";  // No transition during drag
} else {
  article.style.transition = 
    "transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94)";  // Snap animation
}
article.style.transform = `translateX(-${totalOffset}px)`;
```

**Cubic-Bezier Curve:**
- `cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94)` - smooth ease-out
- Creates natural deceleration for page snap

**Drag/Velocity Tracking:** Lines 1543-1694
```typescript
// Velocity calculation (exponential moving average)
const instantVelocity = (touch.clientX - lastTouchXRef.current) / timeDelta
velocityRef.current = 0.6 * instantVelocity + 0.4 * velocityRef.current

// Rubber-band effect at boundaries
if ((atStart && dx > 0) || (atEnd && dx < 0)) {
  const sign = dx > 0 ? 1 : -1
  const absDx = Math.abs(dx)
  offset = sign * Math.sqrt(absDx) * 5  // Square root curve for diminishing resistance
}
```

**Thresholds for Page Turn:**
- Distance threshold: 50px (line 1631)
- Velocity threshold: 0.3 px/ms (line 1632)
- Chapter navigation threshold: 80px overscroll (line 1633)

**Chapter Hint Animation:** Lines 2662-2665
```css
@keyframes pressy-hint-fade-in {
  from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(-50%) scale(0.95); }
  to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(-50%) scale(1); }
}
```
Applied at line 2635 with `0.15s ease-out`

**Footer Animation:** Lines 2668-2686
```css
.pressy-page-footer {
  transform: translateY(100%);
  opacity: 0;
  transition: transform 0.25s ease, opacity 0.25s ease;
}
.pressy-page-footer--visible {
  transform: translateY(0);
  opacity: 1;
}
```

**Other Key Transitions:**
- Navigation arrows opacity: 0.2s ease (line 2588)
- Settings/TOC panels: max-height & opacity 0.25s ease (line 2237, 2781)
- Progress bar width: 0.3s ease (BookProgress.tsx, line 150)

---

### **6. Keyboard and Input Handling**

**Keyboard Navigation:** Lines 1517-1541
- Arrow Right / Space → next page
- Arrow Left → previous page
- Home → first page
- End → last page

**Desktop Click Zones:** Lines 1391-1461
- Left 15% of screen → previous page
- Right 15% of screen → next page
- Middle 70% single click → toggle footer
- Middle 70% double click → toggle fullscreen

**Touch Device Handling:** Lines 1384-1423
- Uses `window.matchMedia("(pointer: coarse)")` to detect touch devices
- Any tap toggles footer (lines 1416-1423)
- Swiping handles page navigation

**Focusability Management:** Lines 1065-1105
- Off-screen elements get `tabindex="-1"` to prevent focus
- Uses `getBoundingClientRect()` accounting for CSS transform
- Restores original tabindex when element comes into view

---

### **7. Chapter Map (Multi-Chapter Loading)**

**Generation:** `/Users/blittle/dev/pressy/packages/pressy/src/vite/plugin.ts` (lines 430-466)
```typescript
export const chapterMap = {
  '01-chapter-slug': () => import('./chapters/01-chapter-slug.mdx'),
  '02-chapter-slug': () => import('./chapters/02-chapter-slug.mdx'),
  // ... etc
}
export const chapterOrder = ['01-chapter-slug', '02-chapter-slug', ...]
```

**Usage in Reader:** Lines 1119-1153
- Accessed via `chapterMapData.chapterMap[nextSlug]()`
- Dynamic imports lazily load chapter components
- Loaded chapters stored in `loadedChapters` state
- Chapter ranges recalculated after load to update page mappings

---

### **8. Key Data Interfaces**

**LoadedChapter** (lines 702-706):
```typescript
interface LoadedChapter {
  slug: string
  title: string
  Content: ComponentType<{ components?: Record<string, unknown> }>
}
```

**ChapterPageRange** (lines 708-712):
```typescript
interface ChapterPageRange {
  slug: string
  startPage: number
  endPage: number
}
```

**ProgressData** (lines 8-14):
```typescript
interface ProgressData {
  page: number
  totalPages: number
  scrollPosition: number
  activeChapterSlug?: string  // In multi-chapter mode
}
```

**ReadingProgress** (types.ts, lines 69-75):
```typescript
interface ReadingProgress {
  chapterSlug: string
  page: number
  totalPages: number
  scrollPosition: number
  timestamp: number
}
```

---

### **9. Configuration**

**Pagination Config:** `/Users/blittle/dev/pressy/packages/pressy/src/config.ts` (lines 9-11)
```typescript
interface PaginationConfig {
  defaultMode: 'scroll' | 'paginated'
}
```

Default: `'scroll'` (line 51)

---

### **Key Implementation Patterns**

1. **Synchronous localStorage writes** for position (always current)
2. **Asynchronous IndexedDB** for history (nice-to-have)
3. **ResizeObserver** for viewport tracking (handles dynamic height)
4. **CSS multi-column layout** for efficient pagination
5. **Cubic-bezier easing** for natural snap feel
6. **Exponential moving average** for smooth velocity calculation
7. **Square-root rubber-band** for boundary resistance
8. **Dynamic import preloading** for seamless chapter transitions

This architecture prioritizes smooth reading experience with reliable progress persistence across session boundaries.

Clean. Here's a summary of all the changes:

---

## Changes Made

### Issue 1: Page Restoration (off-by-one)

Three root causes fixed:

1. **Stale closure in restore effect** — The `.then()` callback captured `totalPages` from the effect's closure, but `totalPages` could change before the promise resolved. Fixed by adding `totalPagesRef` that's always up to date and using `totalPagesRef.current` inside the `.then()`.

2. **Proportional scaling on minor totalPages differences** — When `totalPages` changed by 1-2 pages (due to image/font loading), the proportional scaling `Math.round(fraction * (totalPages - 1))` introduced rounding errors causing off-by-one. Fixed by adding a tolerance: if `totalPages` is within 2 of the saved value, use the exact page number instead of scaling.

3. **Animation on restore** — Added `skipTransitionRef` so the restore jumps instantly to the correct page (no 0.3s CSS transition from page 0 to the saved page). Also added a guard in the `.then()` to bail out if `hasRestoredRef` was already set by another restore path (e.g. `?page=last`).

### Issue 2: Backward Chapter Transition

Two fixes:

1. **No more scroll animation** — The `?page=last` restore now sets `skipTransitionRef.current = true` before `setCurrentPage`, so the translateX effect uses `transition: none` instead of the 0.3s animation. The page appears instantly at the last page.

2. **Content hidden during setup** — Added `awaitingLastPage` state that starts `true` when `?page=last` is detected. The viewport gets `opacity: 0` while waiting, then fades in (`opacity: 1` with `transition: 0.15s`) once the correct page is set. This prevents the brief flash of page 0.

3. **Robust totalPages tracking** — The old `?page=last` effect cleaned up the URL on first match, so later `totalPages` changes (images loading) didn't update the page position. Now uses `wantsLastPageRef` that keeps applying `totalPages - 1` whenever `totalPages` changes, until the user starts interacting (any call to `goToPage` clears the ref).

---

## Summary of all fixes

### Issue 1: Page restoration off-by-one

**Root causes & fixes in `Reader.tsx`:**

1. **Stale closure race condition** — The restore effect's `.then()` used `totalPages` from the closure, which could be stale if `totalPages` changed before the promise resolved. Fixed with `totalPagesRef` that's always current.

2. **Proportional scaling on minor differences** — When `totalPages` differed by just 1-2 pages (image/font loading rounding), proportional scaling introduced rounding errors. Fixed with a tolerance: if within 2 pages, use the exact saved page number.

3. **Animation on restore** — `skipTransitionRef` prevents the 0.3s CSS transition from animating page 0 → saved page on load.

### Issue 2: Backward chapter transition

**Fixes in `Reader.tsx`:**

1. **No scroll animation** — `?page=last` now detected on initial render via `wantsLastPageRef`. Sets `skipTransitionRef = true` before each `setCurrentPage`, so the page jumps instantly.

2. **Content fade-in** — `awaitingLastPage` state starts `true` when `?page=last` detected, setting viewport `opacity: 0`. After the last page is applied, it fades to `opacity: 1` with a 0.15s transition. Prevents the flash of page 0.

3. **Robust totalPages tracking** — Old code cleaned up the URL on first `totalPages` match, so later changes (images loading) didn't update the page. Now uses `wantsLastPageRef` that keeps applying `totalPages - 1` whenever `totalPages` changes, until user interaction (`goToPage` clears the ref).

### Issue 3: Wrong chapter loading on backward navigation

**Root cause:** Going backward from the first chapter navigates to the book's TOC page (`routeType: 'book'`). The `visibilitychange` handler on the old page clears the session flag, so the TOC page's `hydrate()` sees it as a "new session" and auto-redirects to whatever `pressy-last-read` contains — which could be a chapter near the end of the book.

**Fix in `Reader.tsx` + `client.tsx`:** Before any `window.location.href` navigation, set `sessionStorage.setItem('pressy-internal-nav', '1')`. In `hydrate()`, check and consume this flag — skip the auto-redirect if it's present.

Instead, I'll use a linear drag approach that lets the content slide freely without changing the page structure. This is simpler and matches the common pattern in reading apps.

So the approach is to apply a linear offset during the drag when at the start of a chapter with a previous chapter available, letting the content move naturally with the finger.

For the snap-back animation, I need to make sure that when the user doesn't swipe far enough to trigger a chapter change, the content smoothly returns to its starting position. The current setup should handle this—setting `dragOffset` back to 0 and `isDragging` to false will trigger the CSS transition and slide everything back into place. original position with the ease-out animation.

But there might be a visual jump if the drag offset was large (say 200px) and suddenly goes to 0 with a transition. Actually, that's exactly what the transition handles — it animates from the current transform to the new one.

1. During drag: `transition: none`, `transform: translateX(-(0 * vw - 200)) = translateX(200px)` (content shifted right 200px)
2. On release: `isDragging = false`, `dragOffset = 0`
3. Effect runs: `transition: 0.3s ease-out`, `transform: translateX(-(0 * vw - 0)) = translateX(0)` (content at original position)
4. The transition animates from `translateX(200px)` to `translateX(0)`

The snap-back behavior is already built in, so I can move forward with implementing these changes.

content.text:
This does two things in sequence:

1. **`pnpm -r --filter './packages/**' build`** — Builds all packages in topological order (typography → components → pressy). This ensures everything is compiled before starting.

2. **`pnpm -r --parallel --stream --filter './packages/**' --filter 'flatland-example' dev`** — Starts all package watchers (`tsup --watch`) and the flatland dev server (`pressy dev`) in parallel with streamed output.

When you edit a file in `@pressy/components`, tsup rebuilds the dist, then Vite's dev server (used by flatland) picks up the change via HMR.

Run it with:
```
pnpm dev:all
```
